action.reasoning: Observation: Best mIoU is 0.4925 after 10 epochs. Training curves are empty but the pipeline is stable. ~8.4h and 19 iterations remain. The current train.py lacks resume support (the --resume arg exists but isn't used in the visible tail), so a longer run must either start from scratch or I must add resume logic. Analysis: 10 epochs took ~62 min, so 20 more epochs will take ~2h. I will rewrite train.py with: (1) resume from checkpoints/best.pt, (2) cosine LR annealing, (3) gradient clipping, (4) efficient bincount-based validation mIoU, (5) save checkpoint as dict while keeping predict.py backward-compatible. I will also update predict.py to handle dict-style checkpoints. Decision: Write complete train.py and predict.py, syntax-check them, then launch a 20-epoch DDP training run resuming from best.pt.
action.command: python -m py_compile train.py && python -m py_compile predict.py && python -m py_compile model.py && echo 'Syntax OK' && torchrun --nproc_per_node=4 train.py --epochs 20 --resume checkpoints/best.pt --batch_size 4 --lr 1e-4
